findings.description: The artifacts disagree on where system_prompt folding happens. D1 tells SKILL.md to call Agent(prompt=<system_prompt> + separator + <prompt>), while tasks/specs require build_phase_dispatch_kwargs to return a prompt that is already folded. Implementers can easily double-prepend the archetype system prompt or write incompatible tests. Pick one contract: preferably have build-dispatch return the final Agent prompt, and have SKILL.md pass it through unchanged.

apply_phase_outcome is specified as idempotent, but the cache lifecycle makes the second identical call overwrite the previously resolved archetype with null. The first successful call deletes .phase-resolution-cache.json; the second call sees a missing cache, and the spec says missing cache sets phase_archetype=None. That violates the idempotency scenario expecting the cached archetype after both calls. The helper needs a replay rule, such as preserving phase_archetype when last_handoff_id already equals the requested handoff_id.

The status-report JSON schema is incompatible with the existing POST /status/report contract. It requires change_id and phase even though StatusReportRequest defaults both, and it restricts event_type to progress/blocked/completed/failed even though report_status.py currently sends status.phase_transition or status.escalated and archived specs also use gate_check/phase_skip. This contract would reject valid existing clients and misguide implementation tests.

The AgentInfo persistence plan understates the database work. DiscoveryService.discover currently reads through the discover_agents SQL RPC, and heartbeat uses the agent_heartbeat SQL RPC. A migration that only adds a column will not make GET /discovery/agents return phase_archetype and may not persist heartbeat updates unless Python bypasses or updates those RPCs. Add explicit tasks/design steps to update discover_agents output and the heartbeat persistence path.

wp-skills-autopilot does not allow or lock all files its tasks must write. Task 2.9b creates skills/autopilot/scripts/token_budget_check.py and task 2.11 edits .gitignore, but the package write_allow and locks only include phase_agent.py, runner.py, autopilot.py, SKILL.md, and tests. The package will fail scope checking or force out-of-scope edits.

report_status.py currently deduplicates only on phase and change_id. The plan adds phase_archetype to the POST body but does not update the cache key. If a Stop hook reports the same phase before phase_archetype is populated, a later report in the same phase with the resolved archetype will be skipped, so discovery can remain null despite successful resolution.

The PLAN phase scenario contradicts the dispatch matrix. The spec says PLAN continues through /plan-feature and does not receive an explicit Agent dispatch block, but the PLAN scenario still asserts 'the sub-agent dispatch SHALL receive these options verbatim.' That is not testable on the proposed production path unless /plan-feature itself is changed, which is listed as out of scope.

The fallback trigger is inconsistent and tied to the wrong signal. The proposal says AGENT_EXECUTION_ENV indicates whether harness Agent is available, while the design says to inspect tool presence in the system prompt. In this repo AGENT_EXECUTION_ENV is an isolation-profile signal (cloud/local), not a tool-availability flag. Define a deterministic availability contract or leave the check as an orchestrator-level prose guard.

The plan requires PLAN_FIX, IMPL_FIX, and VAL_FIX to record phase_archetype via 'the convergence loop's existing audit path', but convergence_loop.py has no phase_archetype handling and no task adds it. Without explicit convergence-loop wiring, the 'All 13 non-terminal phases' spec will fail for the *_FIX phases.
